title: cephalomanes crispiforme
intro: Cephalomanes crispiforme is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the crisp-leaf filmy fern, which is a delicate, air-rooting fern with crispy and thin leaves. The medicinal properties of Cephalomanes crispiforme include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Cephalomanes crispiforme is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, and it can be found in some herbal remedies and traditional folk medicines.
